top_stories.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
top_stories.summary: Microsoft patched a flaw in the Microsoft Entra ID "Agent ID Administrator" role that could allow privilege escalation and service principal takeover attacks. Security firm Silverfort discovered that the privileged built-in role, designed to manage AI agent identity lifecycle operations, could be exploited for identity takeover.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra Agent ID Flaw Enabled Tenant Takeover via Privilege Escalation
top_stories.summary: A security flaw in Microsoft Entra Agent ID allowed attackers to perform privilege escalation and tenant takeover through Service Principal abuse. Microsoft has fully patched the vulnerability.

top_stories.title: FIDO Alliance wants to keep AI agents from going rogue on online payments
top_stories.summary: The FIDO Alliance has announced a set of initiatives to develop shared standards for AI agent authentication, instruction-following, and transaction execution. The effort addresses how trust and authentication should function when AI agents act on behalf of users in contexts such as online payments and logins.

top_stories.title: Microsoft to roll out Entra passkeys on Windows in late April
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is rolling out passkey support for phishing-resistant passwordless authentication to Microsoft Entra-protected resources from Windows devices starting late April. The update enables passwordless authentication for Entra-connected applications and services directly from Windows devices using passkeys.

top_stories.title: 'Identity is the new battleground': Why your IT helpdesk is suddenly getting a lot of bizarre calls
top_stories.summary: Okta-related vishing attacks are targeting IT helpdesks through impersonation and social engineering tactics that manipulate support staff into granting unauthorized access. Attackers exploit urgency and human trust to bypass authentication controls, enabling rapid escalation into large-scale data breaches.

news.title: The Exchange Online security controls organizations keep getting wrong
news.summary: Microsoft MVP Scott Schnoll discusses common Exchange Online security misconfigurations, focusing on the Shared Responsibility Model and overlooked identity controls. The interview covers Conditional Access policies, Privileged Identity Management (PIM), legacy protocol risks like SMTP AUTH, and audit log blind spots relevant to identity and access management practitioners.

news.title: Identity discovery: The overlooked lever in strategic risk reduction
news.summary: Delinea published an article on identity discovery as a core component of strategic risk reduction for security programs. The piece focuses on how visibility into identities relates to PAM, zero trust, and overall identity program effectiveness.

news.title: Microsoft asks iPhone users to reauthenticate after Outlook outage
news.summary: Microsoft experienced a widespread Outlook.com outage and subsequently required iPhone users to reauthenticate their credentials to restore access via the default iOS Mail app. The issue affected Outlook and Hotmail accounts accessed through Apple's native Mail application following the service disruption.

news.title: Cequence Agent Personas bring granular control and governance to enterprise AI agents
news.summary: Cequence Security has announced general availability of Agent Personas in its AI Gateway product, providing infrastructure-level control over AI agent actions down to individual tool calls. The feature addresses authorization gaps in AI agent deployments using the Model Context Protocol (MCP), where authentication alone does not constrain what authenticated agents are permitted to do.

news.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
news.summary: A research report called "Cyber360: Defending the Digital Battlespace," based on a survey of 500 security professionals, examines how secure data movement creates bottlenecks in Zero Trust program implementation. The article argues that connectivity assumptions undermine Zero Trust initiatives, though the focus appears to be on data movement rather than identity and access management specifically.

news.title: How to fix cybersecurity's agentic AI identity crisis
news.summary: The article addresses security challenges posed by AI agents in enterprise environments, focusing on identity and access management for autonomous AI systems. The piece covers how agentic AI creates new identity security challenges related to authentication, authorization, and access control for non-human identities.

news.title: Can I do that with policy? Understanding the AWS Service Authorization Reference
news.summary: AWS published educational content explaining the AWS Service Authorization Reference and what IAM policies can control within AWS environments. The article covers AWS Organizations service control policies and how to effectively build security controls using AWS IAM policy capabilities.

news.title: Why AI is forcing a reset of the identity stack
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how AI is driving changes in identity and access management, framing a shift from traditional identity management toward "identity intelligence." The article is part of a series exploring the intersection of AI and IAM practices.

news.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
news.summary: The article examines the "AI agent authority gap" in enterprise security, focusing on how AI agents operate as delegated actors rather than independent ones within IAM frameworks. The piece addresses governance challenges around AI agent authorization, continuous observability, and delegation models in enterprise environments.

news.title: DORA and operational resilience: Credential management as a financial risk control
news.summary: The article examines how DORA's Article 9 establishes authentication and access control as legal obligations for EU financial institutions. It covers specific credential management requirements under the regulation and the consequences of control failures.

news.title: The New World ID: Proof of Human for the AI Era Scales Across the Digital Platforms People and Businesses Use Every Day
news.summary: World announced the next generation of World ID, a proof-of-human protocol, with Okta among the partners integrating it alongside Docusign, Tinder, Vercel, and Zoom. The protocol is designed to verify human identity versus AI agents across digital platforms, positioning itself as an identity layer for the AI era.

news.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Help Desk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
news.summary: A threat actor tracked as UNC6692 is conducting social engineering attacks by impersonating IT help desk staff via Microsoft Teams to deploy custom malware called SNOW. The attack method relies on convincing victims to accept Teams chat invitations from fraudulent accounts, a technique relevant to IAM professionals managing identity verification and help desk authentication controls.

news.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
news.summary: The Bitwarden CLI package (@bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0) has been compromised as part of an ongoing supply chain attack discovered by JFrog and Socket. Malicious code was injected into a file called 'bw1.js' within the package, potentially affecting users of the command-line interface for the Bitwarden password manager.

news.title: Regular Password Resets Aren’t as Safe as You Think
news.summary: Specops Software has published research showing how regular password resets create security vulnerabilities by enabling helpdesk social engineering attacks that can lead to full account compromise. The analysis highlights how attackers exploit the password reset process to bypass security controls, targeting a core authentication management workflow.

news.title: Consumer Identity and Access Management Global Market Forecast Report 2026-2036, Profiles of Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, Amazon Web Services, Accenture
news.summary: A market forecast report projects the global Consumer Identity and Access Management (CIAM) market will grow from USD 12.6 billion in 2026 to USD 40.2 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 12.2%. The report covers key vendors including Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, AWS, and Accenture.

news.title: Non-Human Identity Access Management Market Global Forecast Report 2026-2036: Opportunities in Adoption of Zero Trust Architecture, Cloud-native Applications, and Regulatory Compliance Requirements
news.summary: A new market forecast report projects the global non-human identity (NHI) access management market will grow from USD 12.2 billion in 2026 to USD 38.8 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 12.2%. The report covers market dynamics across regions and sectors, with growth drivers including zero trust architecture adoption, cloud-native applications, and regulatory compliance requirements.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in the theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Phishing and MFA exploitation: Targeting the keys to the kingdom
news.summary: A 2025 threat analysis identifies increasing attacker focus on exploiting weaknesses in MFA workflows and using compromised credentials to conduct phishing from trusted accounts. The report centers on trust-based attack vectors that undermine authentication controls, including techniques that bypass or abuse MFA mechanisms.

news.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
news.summary: A Vercel employee's access to an AI tool resulted in a data breach involving stolen OAuth tokens. Researchers highlight OAuth tokens as an emerging primary attack surface and lateral movement vector in modern breaches.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to Microsoft Office users' accounts.
